title: Context Engineering and Repository Grounding · Prompt Defense
summary: Ground an agent in a large unfamiliar repository without flooding its context. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a repository context map and retrieval plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Rank repository files, official docs, generated artifacts, tests, and user statements by authority for the task.
Provide paths, search terms, dependency boundaries, and examples that route efficient discovery.
Separate one-turn constraints from durable repository instructions such as AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: prompt context engineering. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: prompt context engineering. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for refresh drift-prone facts and summarize stable discoveries before context compaction.

content.text: Refresh drift-prone facts and summarize stable discoveries before context compaction. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: State the current model, seek disconfirming evidence, and record what would make the conclusion change.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt context engineering. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Context Refresh, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a repository context map and retrieval plan.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Once loaded, external documentation never needs revalidation. This fails the Context Refresh contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Agent Evaluation Guidance (2026) — Controls multi-turn and tool-using evaluation design for agentic prompts.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Refresh drift-prone facts and summarize stable discoveries before context compaction.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Context Refresh applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refresh drift-prone facts and summarize stable discoveries before context compaction.
